title: Integrity Code Series - Week 6 - Multi-Scale Galvanic Corrosion in Smartphone Charging Ports
description:
Multi-scale galvanic corrosion simulator for USB-C smartphone charging ports under sweat, humidity and pocket-lint electrolyte films. Couples the Laplace equation for electrolyte potential distribution with Butler-Volmer kinetics at each dissimilar metal interface (Au plating, Ni underlayer, stainless shell, Cu traces) under USB Power Delivery bias (5-20V), then layers Faradaic dissolution and oxide film growth over charge cycles. An inverse Bayesian engine recovers exchange current densities from sparse electrochemical impedance data.

The package is the Week 6 entry in the Integrity Code Series of physics-first integrity simulators by Felipe Rocha.
references: Newman J., Thomas-Alyea K.E. (2004). Electrochemical Systems, 3rd ed., Wiley.
Bard A.J., Faulkner L.R. (2001). Electrochemical Methods: Fundamentals and Applications, 2nd ed., Wiley.
Apple Inc. (2024). iPhone 15 USB-C port reliability advisory.
